source: hand-written hp-2rp postcondition-failure tending fixture, corrected by hp-zm7
notes: A push-stale-commits ActionPlan executes successfully (git push returns 0) but the post-execution postcondition that origin/<branch> SHA equals the local <branch> SHA fails — origin still points at the old SHA because of a race with another agent's force-push that arrived between the local commit and our push. Per plan.md §8.3.1 the daemon rolls back the action's cached state ('branch-up-to-date' flag), refuses to mark the bead as pushed, emits a follow-up detection with sourceActionId=ap-001 and severity tied to the failed git.push_branch risk class, emits an activity warning, and the audit log records the precondition pass + postcondition fail + rollback decision regardless of [SILENT] semantics (Guardrail 10). wakeAgent stays false because deterministic rollback covers immediate recovery while the follow-up detection gives the next tend tick restartable input.
